mission.title: Install and Configure Copa on Kubernetes
mission.description: Copa is a CLI tool that allows you to directly patch container images without the need for full rebuilds, addressing critical security vulnerabilities quickly. Installing Copa enables you to enhance your container security posture by applying patches efficiently.

steps.title: Install Copa CLI
steps.description:
Download Copa from GitHub releases:
```bash
# Linux (amd64)
curl -Lo copa https://github.com/project-copacetic/copacetic/releases/download/v0.9.0/copa_0.9.0_linux_amd64
chmod +x copa
sudo mv copa /usr/local/bin/

# macOS (Apple Silicon)
curl -Lo copa https://github.com/project-copacetic/copacetic/releases/download/v0.9.0/copa_0.9.0_darwin_arm64
chmod +x copa
sudo mv copa /usr/local/bin/
```
See all binaries at https://github.com/project-copacetic/copacetic/releases

steps.title: Start buildkitd (required backend)
steps.description:
Copa requires a running buildkitd instance:
```bash
docker run -d --name buildkitd --privileged moby/buildkit:latest
export BUILDKIT_HOST=docker-container://buildkitd
```

steps.title: Scan and patch a container image
steps.description:
Use Trivy to scan for vulnerabilities, then patch with Copa:
```bash
# Scan the image
trivy image --vuln-type os --ignore-unfixed -f json -o scan.json nginx:1.25

# Patch the image
copa patch -i nginx:1.25 -r scan.json -t 1.25-patched
```
This creates a patched image `nginx:1.25-patched` with vulnerabilities fixed.

steps.title: Verify the patched image
steps.description:
Scan the patched image to confirm vulnerabilities are resolved:
```bash
trivy image --vuln-type os nginx:1.25-patched
```

resolution.summary: Copa is installed as a CLI tool for directly patching container image vulnerabilities without full rebuilds.
codeSnippets: trivy image --vuln-type os --ignore-unfixed -f json -o scan.json nginx:1.25

uninstall.title: Remove buildkitd container
uninstall.description:
```bash
docker stop buildkitd && docker rm buildkitd
```

upgrade.title: Download latest Copa release
upgrade.description:
Check https://github.com/project-copacetic/copacetic/releases for the latest version:
```bash
curl -Lo copa https://github.com/project-copacetic/copacetic/releases/latest/download/copa_linux_amd64
chmod +x copa
sudo mv copa /usr/local/bin/
```

troubleshooting.title: buildkitd connection error
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure buildkitd is running and BUILDKIT_HOST is set:
```bash
docker ps | grep buildkitd
export BUILDKIT_HOST=docker-container://buildkitd
```

troubleshooting.title: No patchable vulnerabilities found
troubleshooting.description: Copa can only patch OS-level vulnerabilities with available fixes. Use `--ignore-unfixed` in your Trivy scan to filter.

troubleshooting.title: Unsupported base image
troubleshooting.description: Copa supports Debian, Ubuntu, Alpine, RHEL, and other major distros. Check project docs for the full list.

prerequisites.description: Docker and buildkitd are required. Trivy is recommended for vulnerability scanning. No Kubernetes cluster needed.
